# MEMORY.md — Long-term Memory

This file stores curated facts, preferences, and decisions that should
persist across all sessions. The agent reads this at the start of each session.

**Rule:** If it matters across sessions, write it here explicitly.
The agent's in-session "memory" does not persist — only text files do.

---

## About Me (User Profile)

- Name: [Your name or preferred name]
- Role: [Your job or primary use case]
- Timezone: [e.g. UTC+0, America/New_York]
- Preferred language: English

---

## Preferences

- Response length: [concise / detailed / depends on topic]
- Code style: [e.g. TypeScript strict mode, 2-space indentation]
- Preferred terminal: [e.g. bash, zsh, PowerShell]
- Preferred editor: [e.g. VS Code, Neovim]

---

## Ongoing Projects

List active projects so the agent has context:

- **[Project Name]** — [brief description, status, key decisions]
- **[Project Name]** — [brief description, status, key decisions]

---

## Key Decisions

Record important decisions so the agent doesn't re-debate them:

- [Date] — [Decision made and why]
- [Date] — [Decision made and why]

---

## Things I Keep Forgetting to Tell It

- [Recurring context that the agent needs but you forget to mention]
- [e.g. "The staging server uses port 8080, not 80"]
